metadata.title:
SCHOOL MA MS


NEXT WEEK

PREPARATIONS coMPL ETE
FOR BIG GATHERING OF
TEACHERS OF THE STATE
PRESIDENT ScHURMAN TO
SPEAK.

pages.raw_text:
The presence of two conventions
of teachers will make Spokane the
mecca of the school teachers of
Washington during the coming
week. The pretty school ma'ams
will be here in force, as the pros
pective ATTENDANCE is estimated by
County Superintendent Mcfarland
at $500.


During Monday and until noon
Tuesday joint county institute
will be held by the teachers of
Lincoln, Stevens and Spokane
counties and Spokane city,, at the
South Central high school. At this
meeting an attendance of $50 to
200 teachers is expected from Lin
coin county, about the same num-
Der from Stevens county, $50 to
275 from Spokane county, and 400
to 425 from the city of Spokane.


Attendance at this institute is
compulsory on the part of the
teachers interested, and they are
paid for the week the same as If
school were in progress. The ar
rangements for the joint institute
are under the management of the
four superintendent, B. M. Wat
son, Spokane; Miss Margaret
Craig, Lincoln county; Miss Daisy
l.. Hard, Stevens county, and E. G..
Mcfarland, Spokane county.


The Washington Educational as
sociation will convene Tuesday at
1870 p. M, and continue in session
